title: Workforce planning
summary: 9609 AS — workforce audit, demand/supply forecast, gap analysis, and HR action plans.
learningObjectives: Conduct a basic workforce audit and gap analysis.
Link planning to business strategy and technology change.
Evaluate recruitment, training, or redundancy responses.

simpleExplanation.title: Right people, right time
simpleExplanation.summary: Workforce planning forecasts how many workers and what skills the business will need — then plans recruitment, training, or redundancy to close gaps.
steps: Audit current workforce: skills, age, turnover.
Forecast demand from growth, automation, or decline.
Gap analysis: needed vs available skills/numbers.
Plan recruitment, training, redeployment, or redundancy.

flashcards.back: Process of ensuring business has right number of people with right skills at right time.
Review of current staff — skills, age profile, qualifications, turnover rates.
Compare forecast labour needs with current/supply — identify surpluses or shortages.
Recruit, train existing staff, overtime, outsourcing.
Redundancy, natural wastage, redeployment, hiring freeze.
Retirement wave — succession planning and knowledge transfer needed.
Fewer manual jobs, more technical skills — retraining critical.
Expansion or retrenchment strategy drives workforce forecasts.

sections.content: **Workforce planning** (2.1.2) prevents **skill shortages** and costly **overstaffing**. Case questions often describe **growth or automation** — show the **planning chain**: audit → forecast → gap → action.

sections.question: A bank closes 30 branches and invests in mobile app support. Outline a workforce planning response.
sections.solution:
**Audit:** Branch staff skills — customer service, cash handling; IT team capacity.

**Forecast:** **Fewer branch roles**; **more digital support** and **data/security** skills needed.

**Gap:** Surplus **branch tellers**; shortage **digital agents** and **cyber specialists**.

**Actions:** **Redundancy/redeployment** with retraining packages for some tellers into phone/chat support; **external recruitment** for IT roles; **off-the-job training** (2.1.6) for digital tools — links **6.1.4 technology**.
